text: Zig evaluates generic functions at compile time using comptime parameters, enabling type-safe metaprogramming without runtime overhead.
pgvector is an open-source PostgreSQL extension for storing and querying vector embeddings, supporting exact and approximate nearest neighbor search.
Cosine similarity measures the angle between two vectors, ranging from -1 to 1. It is commonly used to compare text embeddings regardless of magnitude.
Ollama is a tool for running large language models locally. It supports embedding models like nomic-embed-text and mxbai-embed-large for generating vector representations of text.
HNSW is a graph-based approximate nearest neighbor algorithm that provides fast query performance with a tunable speed-recall tradeoff via the ef_search parameter.
The dimensionality of an embedding vector is fixed by the model that produced it. nomic-embed-text outputs 768 dimensions, while mxbai-embed-large outputs 1024.
Zig uses explicit error unions and the try keyword for error propagation. There are no exceptions — errors are values that must be handled or propagated by the caller.
PostgreSQL supports extensions that add functionality beyond the core database. Extensions like pgvector, PostGIS, and pg_trgm are installed with CREATE EXTENSION.
